strengths: Direct experience building and operating event-driven, distributed systems at scale (e.g., Dunnhumby’s micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/sec, GISCollective’s Kubernetes-deployed platform)
Recent hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling (MCP code generators, agentic frameworks) at re:cinq, aligning with the role’s focus on modular multi-agent systems

Strong track record in high-performance backend development (Dlang, C++, Node.js) and systems engineering (observability, caching, async I/O, real-time data processing), critical for fintech-scale agentic AI
concerns: Limited explicit experience with fintech-specific domains (e.g., real-time credit risk, working-capital management) or regulated financial systems, though backend and distributed-systems expertise is highly transferable
No documented experience with foundation-model selection, fine-tuning, or evaluation methodology beyond basic AI-agent tooling, which may require deeper ramp-up in AI-native fintech contexts
No direct evidence of scaling a technical leadership layer to 30+ engineers or managing hybrid engineering models with external partners, though leadership in team growth and dev-ops is demonstrated
response.justification: Bogdan’s resume demonstrates exceptional depth in the core technical prerequisites for this role: building and operating high-scale, event-driven systems (Dunnhumby, GISCollective), recent direct engagement with AI-agent tooling (re:cinq), and disciplined engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development). While his fintech-specific domain experience is not explicit, his background in low-latency, real-time data processing, microservices, and observability stacks provides a direct foundation for adapting to agentic AI in fintech contexts. His leadership in scaling teams and infrastructure, though not in pure fintech, aligns with the role’s emphasis on high-leverage engineering leadership and hybrid delivery models.
